   bool batch_insert_products(const std::vector<Product>& products) {
     // Step 1: Connect
     auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
     if (!conn_result) {
       return false;
     }
     auto conn = *conn_result;

     // Step 2: Drop the table if exists
     auto drop_result = (sqlgen::drop<Product> | sqlgen::if_exists)(conn);
     if (!drop_result) {
       return false;
     }

     // Step 3: Create the table by starting a transaction, inserting an empty vector, and committing.
     auto begin_create_result = conn->begin_transaction();
     if (!begin_create_result) {
       return false;
     }

     auto create_table_result = conn->insert_or_replace(std::vector<Product>{});
     if (!create_table_result) {
       conn->rollback();
       return false;
     }

     auto commit_create_result = conn->commit();
     if (!commit_create_result) {
       conn->rollback();
       return false;
     }

     // Step 4: Begin a transaction for the inserts
     auto begin_insert_result = conn->begin_transaction();
     if (!begin_insert_result) {
       return false;
     }

     // Step 5: Insert the products in batches of 1000
     for (size_t i = 0; i < products.size(); i += 1000) {
       size_t batch_size = std::min<size_t>(1000, products.size() - i);
       std::vector<Product> batch(products.begin() + i, products.begin() + i + batch_size);

       auto insert_result = conn->insert_or_replace(batch);
       if (!insert_result) {
         conn->rollback();
         return false;
       }
     }

     // Step 6: Commit the transaction
     auto commit_result = conn->commit();
     if (!commit_result) {
       conn->rollback();
       return false;
     }

     return true;
   }

  Code:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

/**
 * description: replace_text_in_notes.
 * This function demonstrates how to replace text in notes using sqlgen update API.
 * It replaces all occurrences of "foo" with "bar" in the content column of the Note table.
 * Returns: std::vector<UpdatedNote>.
 */
std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Build the update statement: set content to replace(content, "foo", "bar")
  auto update_stmt = sqlgen::update<Note>(sqlgen::col<"content">.set(sqlgen::replace(sqlgen::col<"content">, "foo", "bar")));

  // Execute the update
  update_stmt(conn);

  // Read the updated notes
  auto notes = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Note>>(conn).value();

  // Transform to UpdatedNote
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> updated_notes;
  for (const auto& note : notes) {
    updated_notes.push_back(UpdatedNote{note.note_id, note.content});
  }

  return updated_notes;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes
```
